## Additional thoughts on addition

Pun intended $\text{[wink:1.5em]{.twemoji}}$.


In a way, counting and adding up are the same thing. Each of the counting numbers can be obtained by adding $\color{blue}{1}$ to the $\color{red}{\text{previous}}$ one.

```{.tikz fig-align="center"}
\begin{tikzpicture}[>=stealth, x=1.5cm, every node/.style={font=\Large}]
  % Define the numbers
  \node (n0) at (0,0) {$0$};
  \node (n1) at (1,0) {$1$};
  \node (n2) at (2,0) {$2$};
  \node (n3) at (3,0) {$3$};
  \node (n4) at (4,0) {$4$};
  \node (ndots) at (5,0) {$\dots$};
  \node (nm1) at (6,0) {$n-1$};
  \node (nn) at (7,0) {$n$};
  
  % Draw the curved arrows with +1
  \foreach \i/\j in {n0/n1, n1/n2, n2/n3, n3/n4, n4/ndots, ndots/nm1, nm1/nn} {
    \draw[->, blue, thick] (\i) to[out=60,in=120] node[above, text=blue] {\footnotesize $+1$} (\j);
  }
\end{tikzpicture}
```

That means that if we start with $0$, the next number is ${\color{red}{0}} + \color{blue}{1}$, the next one is ${\color{red}{1}} + \color{blue}{1}$,  and so on.


```{.tikz fig-align="center"}
\begin{tikzpicture}[>=stealth]
  \matrix (m) [matrix of math nodes, nodes={font=\Large}, row sep=0.5em, column sep=0.15em] {
      0 &   &                 &   &               \\
      1 & = & \color{red}{0} & + & \color{blue}{1} \\
      2 & = & \color{red}{1} & + & \color{blue}{1} \\
      3 & = & \color{red}{2} & + & \color{blue}{1} \\
      4 & = & \color{red}{3} & + & \color{blue}{1} \\
      \vdots & & \vdots       &   & \vdots         \\
      n & = & \color{red}{n-1} & + & \color{blue}{1} \\
  };
  \draw[->, gray, shorten >= 2pt, shorten <= 2pt] (m-1-1) -- (m-2-3);
  \draw[->, gray, shorten >= 2pt, shorten <= 2pt] (m-2-1) -- (m-3-3);
  \draw[->, gray, shorten >= 2pt, shorten <= 2pt] (m-3-1) -- (m-4-3);
  \draw[->, gray, shorten >= 2pt, shorten <= 2pt] (m-4-1) -- (m-5-3);
\end{tikzpicture}
```

We can say that the counting numbers are **generated by** $\color{blue}{1}$. In other words, there is a _successor_ function that takes a number and gives you the next one by adding $1$.

:::{.big-math}
$$
n \to \text{[top_hat:1.0em]{.twemoji}} \to n + \color{blue}{1}
$$
:::
